text.p: Following presentations made to it, the Board of Regents has approved a new salary structure tor the University and also approved increases in pay volume for faculty and staff.

The effective date for the new structure and the new pay is 1st July 2005 as against the 1st October incremental date. However, migration from current salary structure to the new one for serving faculty is based on following three factors.
i) Graduated increase to reflect supply inelastic disciplines and supply inelastic status beginning with any Lecturer I and above who has a Ph.D. This is applied across discipline.
ii) Performance assessment graduated increase for every regular staff recently appraised.
iii) Cots of living pay rise for every faculty on regular appointment.
On recommendation of Management, the Chancellor has approved the outcome of the consideration under above criteria as they concern you and in line with the inputed grades.
Consequently, you have been relocated to the new salary package on . This amounts to a monthly salary of N .
Please note that this review and the pay increase was carried out essentially to:
- Motivate you into greater productivity in your core assignment of teaching, research and community development.
- Close financial gap suggested by living cost demands.

It is hoped that this increase should galvanize you to a higher productivity, greater commitment to your students as well as generate an inner drive and a capacity-building urge that should turn you to a global amazement in your discipline.
